headline: Belarus and Russia Launch Joint Tactical-Nuclear Drills on Day One of Warsh's Chairmanship as Wall Street Slides on Iran/Oil; OpenAI Plugs ChatGPT Pro into 12,000 Banks via Plaid; Bloomberg/PBS Confirm Anthropic Co-Founder Christopher Olah Will Stand with Pope Leo XIV at the May 25 'Magnifica Humanitas' Unveiling — and Google I/O Opens Today with a New Gemini in the Gravity of GPT-5.5 and Claude Mythos
summary: Monday May 18 turned the page on three storylines at once. In Minsk, Belarus's defence ministry said missile units and the air force would practice the delivery of tactical nuclear weapons from 'unprepared locations' jointly with Russia — Kyiv condemned the drills as an 'unprecedented challenge,' and Tsikhanouskaya warned Lukashenko has 'turned Belarus into a target.' In Washington, Kevin Warsh's first full Monday as 17th Fed chair coincided with a 1%+ slide across the Dow (-537pts), S&P (-1.2%), Nasdaq (-1.5%, NVDA -4.4%) and a -2.3% BTC drop to $76,548 as Brent ticked to $108/bbl on stalled US-Iran talks and CNBC strategists warning Gulf inventories may not normalise until December 2027. In San Francisco, OpenAI took ChatGPT Pro live with Plaid-managed connections to 12,000+ US financial institutions (Schwab, Fidelity, Chase, Robinhood, AmEx, Capital One); in the same Monday news cycle Bloomberg, PBS, NCR and America Magazine reported Anthropic co-founder Christopher Olah will personally stand beside Pope Leo XIV at the May 25 unveiling of Magnifica Humanitas in the Vatican's Synod Hall — a frontier-lab presence at a papal magisterial event with no real precedent. Today Tuesday May 19, Google I/O's 10am-PT keynote opens with a new Gemini expected to land in the gravity of GPT-5.5 and short of Claude Mythos, alongside Android XR for smart glasses and an 'agentic AI for Android' push.

highlights.title: Belarus + Russia Launch Joint Tactical-Nuclear Drills from 'Unprepared Locations' — Oreshnik Now in Service in Belarus, Kyiv Calls It an 'Unprecedented Challenge,' Tsikhanouskaya Warns Lukashenko Has 'Turned Belarus into a Target'
highlights.summary: Belarus's defence ministry announced on Monday May 18 that its missile units and air force would conduct joint snap exercises with Russia practicing the use and delivery of tactical nuclear weapons from 'unprepared locations' across the country — exercises that include preparing the weapons for use, not just rehearsing logistics. The drills come months after Russia announced in December that its intermediate-range Oreshnik nuclear-capable missile system had formally entered service in Belarus, on a Belarusian map that borders Ukraine and three NATO members (Poland, Latvia, Lithuania). Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said the drills confirm Russia is 'trying to draw Belarus deeper into the war,' Kyiv's foreign ministry called the exercises an 'unprecedented challenge to global security,' and Belarusian opposition leader Sviatlana Tsikhanouskaya said the deployment of Russian nuclear weapons has 'turned Belarus into a target.' The drills land into the same Reuters/Bloomberg read of the Trump-Zelensky written peace proposal — still 90–95% agreed, Russian sign-off the only gap — and into the second Trump-administration renewal of the US oil-sanctions waiver for Russia despite earlier statements that no further extension would be granted.

highlights.title: OpenAI Launches Personal Finance in ChatGPT Pro (US Preview) — Plaid-Managed Read-Only Links to 12,000+ Institutions Including Schwab, Fidelity, Chase, Robinhood, AmEx and Capital One; Dashboard Covers Portfolio, Spending, Subscriptions, Upcoming Payments
highlights.summary: OpenAI rolled out a preview of Personal Finance in ChatGPT for Pro users in the United States — initially limited to a 'small group of users on web and iOS,' with plans to extend to ChatGPT Plus and eventually all users. Account connections are managed through Plaid, with read-only access to balances, transactions, investments and liabilities across more than 12,000 US financial institutions, including Schwab, Fidelity, Chase, Robinhood, American Express and Capital One. ChatGPT cannot see full account numbers and cannot move money. Once accounts are connected, the user gets a dashboard covering portfolio performance, spending, subscriptions and upcoming payments, plus the ability to ask grounded questions about their finances. Intuit support is reported as 'coming soon.' Background: OpenAI acquired the team behind personal-finance startup Hiro (backed by Ribbit, General Catalyst, Restive) in April. In parallel, The AI Insider reported that Greg Brockman is consolidating ChatGPT product surface area under a single org, a structural read that pairs with the personal-finance launch's tone of 'making ChatGPT the agent-of-record for personal data.'

highlights.title: Warsh Day-One Monday Slide: Dow -537 / -1.1%, S&P -1.2% to 7,408.50, Nasdaq -1.5% to 26,225.15, NVDA -4.4%, BTC -2.3% to $76,548; Brent $108/bbl on Stalled US-Iran Talks and CNBC Strategists Warning Gulf Stocks May Not Normalise Until December 2027
highlights.summary: Kevin Warsh's first full Monday as 17th chair of the Federal Reserve was met by a broad equity drawdown that pushed traders' implied path further away from the cuts Trump appointed him to deliver. The Dow closed -1.1% (-537.29 pts) at 49,526.17; the S&P 500 -1.2% (-92.74) at 7,408.50; the Nasdaq Composite -1.5% at 26,225.15 — with NVIDIA the Dow's worst single name at -4.4% as oil-led inflation risk re-priced. Bitcoin dropped -2.32% to $76,548.57 in tight correlation with tech beta. Brent settled around $108/bbl as US-Iran negotiations were described as 'at a standstill' and CNBC's Monday strategist round-up warned the Gulf inventory hole may not normalise until December 2027 — with a slower path implying Brent toward $150 'staying elevated for the rest of the year.' Powell remains on the Fed board as a governor through January 2028; Warsh's first FOMC as chair is on the June 16–17 calendar.

Trump Renews Russia Oil-Sanctions Waiver for the Second Time, Reversing Earlier Public 'No Further Extension' Posture — Quiet Continuation of Channel as Belarus Drills Open and Trump-Zelensky Proposal Still Stuck at 90–95%

perspectives.title: Lukashenko's Nuclear Theatre vs Kyiv's Counter-Frame: Drill as Negotiating Asset, or Drill as Self-Targeting?
perspectives.subtitle: Bloomberg/AP and the Belarusian opposition read the same exercises in opposite directions — Minsk and Moscow frame the drills as Article-5 deterrence outside a still-90–95% peace text; Kyiv and Tsikhanouskaya frame them as evidence that the deeper Belarus is dragged into the war, the more it becomes a legitimate target.

sources.name: Belarus Ministry of Defence / Lukashenko government readout — May 18, via Bloomberg and AP wire
sources.quote: Missile units and the air force will practice the use and delivery of nuclear weapons in unprepared locations across the country. In cooperation with the Russian side it is planned to practice the delivery of nuclear weapons and preparations for their use. This is Article-5-equivalent deterrence inside the Union State, sized to the reality that the Oreshnik intermediate-range nuclear-capable system formally entered service in Belarus in December — and is a measured response to the ongoing peace text being negotiated without Minsk in the room.

sources.name: President Zelensky / Ukrainian foreign ministry + Sviatlana Tsikhanouskaya — May 18 readouts
sources.quote: Russia is trying to draw Belarus deeper into the war. These drills are an unprecedented challenge to global security and they are designed to coerce the negotiation, not to defend Belarus. The deployment of Russian nuclear weapons has turned Belarus into a target — every step Lukashenko takes here narrows his own country's strategic optionality, not Ukraine's.

perspectives.title: ChatGPT's New Plaid Plumbing vs. the Vatican's New Frontier-Lab Adjacency: Two Monday Stories That Re-Stage Where 'AI Safety' Now Lives
perspectives.subtitle: On the same Monday, OpenAI moved ChatGPT into the read layer of US household finances and Anthropic moved its co-founder onto the dais of the first papal AI encyclical — two opposite directions of legitimacy capture, both happening inside one news cycle.

sources.name: OpenAI launch blog + TechCrunch / American Banker — May 15–18 rollout reporting
sources.quote: We're opening a preview of Personal Finance in ChatGPT for Pro users in the US, with read-only Plaid-managed connections across more than 12,000 financial institutions. ChatGPT cannot see full account numbers and cannot move money — but it can see balances, transactions, investments and liabilities, and answer grounded questions on top of them. The trust posture is technical: scoped permissions, read-only by design, an explicit dashboard layer the user controls.

sources.name: Bloomberg / PBS / National Catholic Reporter / America Magazine — May 18 confirmations
sources.quote: Anthropic co-founder Christopher Olah will stand beside Pope Leo XIV at the 11:30am presentation of Magnifica Humanitas in the Vatican's Synod Hall on May 25. The Pope's personal participation in the launch of his own encyclical is atypical and signals where the Vatican wants its voice to live in the AI debate — and the choice of an Anthropic co-founder rather than an OpenAI, Google or Microsoft principal as the lab-side face of the moment is itself a piece of legitimacy positioning that the Vatican will have weighed.

quotes.text: The deployment of Russian nuclear weapons has turned Belarus into a target. This is not deterrence — it is co-option, and every Belarusian family is now living inside the consequence of one man's decisions.
quotes.source: Tsikhanouskaya office statement on Belarus-Russia joint nuclear drills, May 18 — via AP and Bloomberg wires

quotes.text: Russia is trying to draw Belarus deeper into the war. These drills are an unprecedented challenge to global security. They do not change the written proposal — they tell us why the written proposal needs to be signed.
quotes.source: Office of the President of Ukraine — May 18 reaction to Belarus-Russia tactical-nuclear drills

quotes.text: Markets will price what they wish to price. The Federal Reserve will continue to make its decisions on the basis of incoming data and the dual mandate set by Congress — independence is a process, not a posture.
quotes.source: Warsh's first day-one statement to staff at the Eccles Building — May 18 readout via Reuters and CNBC

event_updates.title: Belarus + Russia Launch Joint Tactical-Nuclear Drills (May 18) — Trump Quietly Renews Russia Oil-Sanctions Waiver for the Second Time; Ukraine Claims Stepnohirsk; Trump-Zelensky Written Proposal Still 90–95% Agreed
event_updates.latest_summary: Belarus's defence ministry announced Monday May 18 that missile units and the air force will jointly practice the use and delivery of tactical nuclear weapons with Russian forces from 'unprepared locations' across Belarus — months after the Oreshnik intermediate-range nuclear-capable system formally entered service there. Ukraine's foreign ministry called it an 'unprecedented challenge,' Zelensky said Moscow is 'trying to draw Belarus deeper into the war,' and Sviatlana Tsikhanouskaya said the deployment has 'turned Belarus into a target.' In parallel, the Trump administration quietly renewed the Russia oil-sanctions waiver for the second time despite earlier statements that no further extension would be granted; Ukrainian forces claim Stepnohirsk in Zaporizhzhia oblast with HUR releasing 'Artan' special-unit footage; the Reuters/Bloomberg read of the written peace text remains 90–95% agreed.

event_updates.title: May 25 Synod-Hall Unveiling of 'Magnifica Humanitas' Confirmed by Vatican News + Bloomberg / PBS / NCR / America Magazine — Anthropic Co-Founder Christopher Olah Will Stand with Pope Leo XIV at 11:30am
event_updates.latest_summary: Vatican News confirmed Monday May 18 that Pope Leo XIV will publish his first encyclical, 'Magnifica Humanitas: On the Protection of Human Dignity in the Age of Artificial Intelligence,' on May 25 — signed dated May 15 (Rerum Novarum 135th anniversary) and presented at 11:30am in the Synod Hall. In parallel, Bloomberg, PBS, NCR, America Magazine, Washington Times and Angelus all reported that Anthropic co-founder Christopher Olah will join the Pope at the launch — a frontier-lab founder physically present on the dais of a papal magisterial event, without real precedent. Vatican framing pairs the AI encyclical with the May 17–24 Laudato Si' Week as continuous Catholic social teaching from the industrial age to the AI age.

event_updates.title: 12th Week of Effective Hormuz Closure; CNBC Monday Round-Up Warns Gulf Stocks May Not Normalise Until December 2027 with Brent Risk Toward $150
event_updates.latest_summary: Brent ticked to ~$108/bbl Monday May 18 as US-Iran negotiations were described as 'at a standstill.' CNBC's strategist round-up warned global oil stockpiles are 'plummeting' and may not recover until December 2027, with a slower-reopening path implying Brent toward $150 and 'staying elevated for the rest of the year.' Sits inside Trump's broader Monday news cycle that included a second renewal of the Russia oil-sanctions waiver — i.e., the administration is making the Russia barrel easier to clear at the same moment the Gulf barrel is being kept harder to clear.

event_updates.title: OpenAI Pulls ChatGPT Pro into the Read Layer of US Household Finances (Plaid, 12,000+ Institutions, Read-Only) — Greg Brockman Reportedly Consolidating ChatGPT Product Surface Area Under One Org
event_updates.latest_summary: OpenAI rolled out a US-Pro preview of Personal Finance in ChatGPT on May 15–18, with Plaid-managed read-only connections to 12,000+ financial institutions (Schwab, Fidelity, Chase, Robinhood, AmEx, Capital One). ChatGPT cannot see full account numbers and cannot move money. The launch follows OpenAI's April acquisition of the Hiro team (Ribbit, General Catalyst, Restive); The AI Insider's May 18 brief reports Greg Brockman consolidating ChatGPT product surface area under a single org — the structural read that pairs naturally with this 'agent-of-record for personal data' move.
